title: VILON
description: Vilon is a synthetic dipeptide bioregulator consisting of lysine and glutamic acid (Lys-Glu) that acts as an immunomodulator and geroprotective agent. It primarily functions by activating gene expression through chromatin deheterochromatinization, enhancing T-cell differentiation, and stimulating digestive enzyme activity. Vilon has been studied for applications in cancer therapy, immune system support, anti-aging interventions, and improving intestinal function in elderly populations.

research.title: Bioregulator Vilon-induced reactivation of chromatin in cultured lymphocytes from old people
research.summary: Vilon induced chromatin deheterochromatinization in lymphocytes from elderly individuals, reactivating ribosomal genes and releasing repressed genes while preserving structural heterochromatin integrity.

research.title: Effect of vilon on biological age and lifespan in mice
research.summary: Subcutaneous Vilon administration increased physical activity and endurance, prolonged lifespan, and prevented spontaneous neoplasm development in female CBA mice without adverse effects.

research.title: Application of peptide bioregulator in complex treatment of elderly cancer patients
research.summary: Vilon inclusion in cancer treatment schemes increased 2-year survival rates, prevented post-operative complications, and improved quality of life in elderly patients with stage III rectal and colon cancer.

research.title: Immunomodulating effects of Vilon and its analogue in the culture of human and animal thymus cells
research.summary: Vilon increased CD5 lymphocyte differentiation marker expression in thymic cells and induced T-cell precursor differentiation towards CD4+ T-helpers, demonstrating immunomodulatory and antiallergic activity.

research.title: Effects of short peptides on lymphocyte chromatin in senile subjects
research.summary: Vilon activated ribosome genes, induced chromatin decondensation, and released genes repressed by age-specific heterochromatinization in leukocytes of individuals aged 75-88 years.

research.title: Effect of the dipeptide vilon on activity of digestive enzyme in rats of various ages
research.summary: Oral Vilon administration increased digestive enzyme activity in the gastrointestinal tract, with most pronounced effects in 11-month-old rats, diminishing age-related differences in enzyme function.

research.title: Effects of short peptides on thymocyte blast transformation and signal transduction along the sphingomyelin pathway
research.summary: Vilon produced the most potent comitogenic effect on thymocyte proliferation and significantly stimulated sphingomyelinase activity in mouse thymocyte membranes compared to other peptides tested.

research.title: Immunohistochemical and morphometric analysis of effects of vilon and epithalon on functional morphology of radiosensitive organs
research.summary: Vilon stimulated thymocyte proliferative activity and enhanced stem cell proliferative potential in the intestine, promoting post-radiation recovery of critical organs in irradiated rats.

sentiment.summary: Limited English-language user discussions on Vilon. This Khavinson peptide is primarily used in Russia and Eastern European countries for immune system support.

effects.label: Anti-Aging Applications:
effects.description: Effectively reverses age-related gene silencing and chromatin condensation in elderly subjects aged 75-88 years

effects.label: Cancer Therapy:
effects.description: Improved 2-year survival rates and quality of life when added to treatment regimens for elderly stage III colon/rectal cancer patients

effects.label: Radiation Protection:
effects.description: Stimulates post-radiation recovery of thymus and intestinal tissue in animal models exposed to 6 Gy gamma-irradiation

effects.label: Age-Related Improvement:
effects.description: Most pronounced benefits observed in older animals and humans, diminishing age-related functional differences

dosage.label: Research Dosing:
dosage.description: 1 mg/kg subcutaneous injection used in animal longevity studies with positive outcomes

dosage.label: Cancer Treatment Protocol:
dosage.description: Applied as immunomodulator addition to standard radio/chemotherapy regimens in clinical oncology settings

dosage.label: Oral Administration:
dosage.description: Given per os (orally) for 1 month in studies examining digestive enzyme enhancement

dosage.label: Culture Concentrations:
dosage.description: 5 ng/ml effective dose in tissue culture studies for stimulating apoptosis and cell proliferation

sideEffects.label: Drug Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Combined use with cyclophosphamide decreased survival in tumor-bearing mice; synchronous use with cytostatic drugs not recommended

sideEffects.label: Minimal Adverse Effects:
sideEffects.description: Long-term administration showed no unfavorable effects on animal development in chronic studies

sideEffects.label: Tissue Specificity:
sideEffects.description: Effects are tissue-specific; primarily stimulates thymic tissue rather than having systemic broad-spectrum activity

sideEffects.label: Safety Profile:
sideEffects.description: No significant side effects reported in elderly cancer patients or aged subjects in clinical trials

availability.label: Research Status:
availability.description: Primarily available as research peptide; studied extensively in Russian medical institutions since early 2000s

availability.label: Clinical Use:
availability.description: Applied in specialized oncology and gerontology settings in Russia as adjuvant therapy for elderly cancer patients

availability.label: Synthetic Production:
availability.description: Manufactured as synthetic dipeptide (Lys-Glu) for consistent pharmaceutical quality and research applications
